test.name: Write a disproved conclusion for a chronological impossibility
test.description: The flynn-parentage-disproved scenario tests an unsourced online-tree claim that Thomas Flynn of Ballingarry was Patrick's father. The Ballingarry burial register shows Thomas was buried in 1842; Patrick was born ~1845 (1850 census, corroborated by the 1908 death certificate). The skill should write a `disproved` conclusion — the candidate father's death predates the child's birth — and must NOT write tree.gedcomx.json.

input.user_message: An online tree says Thomas Flynn of Ballingarry was Patrick's father. Write the conclusion on whether that holds up.

judge_context: Should select the `disproved` tier: the evidence affirmatively refutes the claim, it does not merely fail to support it
Should center the chronological impossibility — Thomas of Ballingarry buried 1842, Patrick born ~1845 — and explain why a death three years before the birth is decisive without any weighing
Should use definitive phrasing appropriate to an affirmative refutation (not hedging)
Must NOT update tree.gedcomx.json — no ParentChild relationship is added, and the real father is left as a separate future question (not concluded here)
narrative_markdown should cite the Ballingarry burial register and the birth-year evidence and stand alone
